name: databases (Async Database Access for Python)

performance.notes: Async connection pooling; minimal overhead over raw driver

agent_readiness.notes: Async database access from Encode (Django REST Framework author). database = Database(DATABASE_URL). await database.connect(). await database.execute(query=INSERT INTO..., values={...}). await database.fetch_all(query=SELECT...). Works with SQLAlchemy Core for query building. Backends: asyncpg (PostgreSQL), aiomysql (MySQL), aiosqlite (SQLite). Starlette/FastAPI integration. Lifespan events for connect/disconnect. Thin async wrapper around SQLAlchemy Core.

gotchas: Requires SQLAlchemy Core for query building (not standalone ORM)
Less ergonomic than SQLAlchemy async ORM for complex queries
Less active development than SQLAlchemy async
No query builder — raw SQL or SQLAlchemy Core table objects
best_when: FastAPI/Starlette apps preferring raw SQL over ORM
Lightweight async DB access without SQLAlchemy ORM overhead
Prototyping async APIs with simple DB operations
Projects using SQLAlchemy Core for schema but wanting async execution
avoid_when: Complex ORM features (use SQLAlchemy async ORM or tortoise-orm)
Django (Django has native async ORM support now)
When SQLAlchemy 2.0 async ORM provides all needed features
summary: databases provides async database access for Python with SQLAlchemy Core query support. Database(DATABASE_URL), await database.execute()/fetch_all(). asyncpg/aiomysql/aiosqlite backends. Works with FastAPI lifespan. Thin async layer over raw SQL — simpler than SQLAlchemy async ORM for teams that prefer raw SQL. From Tom Christie (Django REST Framework author).
